[[JA:志紀長吉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852498}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Shikinagayoshi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:志紀長吉神社 11月.jpg|250px|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Hirano-ku|Q861256|WD=Q861256|1=Hirano-ku, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Nagayoshi Nagahara|Q88286573|WD=Q88286573|1=Nagayoshi-nagahara}}2-8-23 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Nagae Sotsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 13 ({{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (parallel major), former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |札所等= |本殿= |例祭= October 16 |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shiki
Nagayoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Shikinagayoshi Shrine''' (しきながよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagayoshi Nagahara|Q88286573|WD=Q88286573|1=Nagayoshi-nagahara}}, {{ill|lt=Hirano-ku|Q861256|WD=Q861256|1=Hirano-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It was formerly a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === Founded in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 13 ({{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}). When they worshiped {{ill|lt=Lycopodium clavatum|Q466426|WD=Q466426|lt=hikagekazura|1=Lycopodium clavatum}} growing east of this shrine, {{ill|lt=Emperor Heizei|Q353558|WD=Q353558|1=Emperor Heizei}} granted the divine title Hikage Daimyōjin and the imperial poem "Kamiyama no hikage no tsura kazatefu toyo no akari no wakete kumanaki." Since then, hikagekazura has been the shrine crest. It was later designated an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, became a prayer shrine of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Suzaku|Q349296|WD=Q349296|1=Emperor Go-Suzaku}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Reizei|Q356060|WD=Q356060|1=Emperor Go-Reizei}}, and Amida-ji was founded as its shrine temple. In the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} and {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, it was called Nagahara Ōmiya. On May 6, {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}), after the {{ill|lt=Battle of Dōmyōji|Q1002716|WD=Q1002716|1=Battle of Dōmyōji}}, {{ill|lt=Sanada Nobushige|Q3948081|WD=Q3948081|1=Sanada Yukimura}} stopped at this shrine, prayed for victory, and rested at its horse grounds. At that time, a banner and sword were dedicated to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was mainly called Hikage Daimyōjin. It enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and drew worship for reputed efficacy in rain-prayer rituals. When {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} was carried out in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, the shrine temple Amida-ji was abolished, but this shrine retained the materials without dispersal. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The sword dedicated by Sanada Nobushige was confiscated by {{ill|lt=GHQ|Q471889|WD=Q471889|lt=GHQ|1=Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers}} after {{ill|lt=World War II|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Nagae Sotsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} File:志紀長吉神社 祭神・由緒.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine deities and history === Precinct=== * Honden * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Kotohira-gū|Q94760|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Jinmu Emperor's Mausoleum (Unebi NE mausoleum)|UNKNOWN|lt=Mausoleum of Emperor Jinmu at Unebiyama}} * Shrine office * Mikoshi storehouse File:志紀長吉神社 琴平社.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine Kotohira Shrine File:志紀長吉神社 稲荷社.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine Inari Shrine File:志紀長吉神社 遥拝所.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine distant worship site === Cultural properties === === Osaka City designated tangible cultural properties === * Silk painting of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=Kūkai}} * Silk painting Gozu Tennō mandala * 11 documents of Shikinagayoshi Shrine === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Nagahara Station (Osaka)|Q151508|WD=Q151508|lt=Nagahara Station|1=Nagahara Station (Osaka)}} on {{ill|lt=Osaka Metro|Q51211653|WD=Q51211653|1=Osaka Metro (company)}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|Q1142773|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} === Bibliography === * Shikinagayoshi Shrine origin record === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.shikinagayoshi-jinjya.org/ Shikinagayoshi Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:平野区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:長吉]]

== name in kana (P1814) == * しきながよしじんじゃ == Commons category (P373) == * Shikinagayoshi-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|WD=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '二丁目8番23号', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '大阪市平野区長吉長原', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Shikinagayoshi-jinja}} [[JA:志紀長吉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852498}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Shikinagayoshi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:志紀長吉神社 11月.jpg|250px|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Hirano-ku|Q861256|WD=Q861256|1=Hirano-ku, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Nagayoshi Nagahara|Q88286573|WD=Q88286573|1=Nagayoshi-nagahara}}2-8-23 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Nagae Sotsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 13 ({{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (parallel major), former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |札所等= |本殿= |例祭= October 16 |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shiki
Nagayoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Shikinagayoshi Shrine''' (しきながよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagayoshi Nagahara|Q88286573|WD=Q88286573|1=Nagayoshi-nagahara}}, {{ill|lt=Hirano-ku|Q861256|WD=Q861256|1=Hirano-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It was formerly a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === Founded in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 13 ({{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}). When they worshiped {{ill|lt=Lycopodium clavatum|Q466426|WD=Q466426|lt=hikagekazura|1=Lycopodium clavatum}} growing east of this shrine, {{ill|lt=Emperor Heizei|Q353558|WD=Q353558|1=Emperor Heizei}} granted the divine title Hikage Daimyōjin and the imperial poem "Kamiyama no hikage no tsura kazatefu toyo no akari no wakete kumanaki." Since then, hikagekazura has been the shrine crest. It was later designated an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, became a prayer shrine of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Suzaku|Q349296|WD=Q349296|1=Emperor Go-Suzaku}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Reizei|Q356060|WD=Q356060|1=Emperor Go-Reizei}}, and Amida-ji was founded as its shrine temple. In the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} and {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, it was called Nagahara Ōmiya. On May 6, {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}), after the {{ill|lt=Battle of Dōmyōji|Q1002716|WD=Q1002716|1=Battle of Dōmyōji}}, {{ill|lt=Sanada Nobushige|Q3948081|WD=Q3948081|1=Sanada Yukimura}} stopped at this shrine, prayed for victory, and rested at its horse grounds. At that time, a banner and sword were dedicated to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was mainly called Hikage Daimyōjin. It enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and drew worship for reputed efficacy in rain-prayer rituals. When {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} was carried out in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, the shrine temple Amida-ji was abolished, but this shrine retained the materials without dispersal. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The sword dedicated by Sanada Nobushige was confiscated by {{ill|lt=GHQ|Q471889|WD=Q471889|lt=GHQ|1=Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers}} after {{ill|lt=World War II|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Nagae Sotsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} File:志紀長吉神社 祭神・由緒.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine deities and history === Precinct=== * Honden * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Kotohira-gū|Q94760|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Jinmu Emperor's Mausoleum (Unebi NE mausoleum)|UNKNOWN|lt=Mausoleum of Emperor Jinmu at Unebiyama}} * Shrine office * Mikoshi storehouse File:志紀長吉神社 琴平社.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine Kotohira Shrine File:志紀長吉神社 稲荷社.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine Inari Shrine File:志紀長吉神社 遥拝所.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine distant worship site === Cultural properties === === Osaka City designated tangible cultural properties === * Silk painting of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=Kūkai}} * Silk painting Gozu Tennō mandala * 11 documents of Shikinagayoshi Shrine === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Nagahara Station (Osaka)|Q151508|WD=Q151508|lt=Nagahara Station|1=Nagahara Station (Osaka)}} on {{ill|lt=Osaka Metro|Q51211653|WD=Q51211653|1=Osaka Metro (company)}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|Q1142773|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} === Bibliography === * Shikinagayoshi Shrine origin record === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.shikinagayoshi-jinjya.org/ Shikinagayoshi Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:平野区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:長吉]]

[[JA:志紀長吉神社]] {{wikidata link|Q106852498}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称= Shikinagayoshi Shrine |画像 = [[画像:志紀長吉神社 11月.jpg|250px|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]]
Torii and haiden |所在地= {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Hirano-ku|Q861256|WD=Q861256|1=Hirano-ku, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Nagayoshi Nagahara|Q88286573|WD=Q88286573|1=Nagayoshi-nagahara}}2-8-23 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Nagae Sotsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}、{{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} |創建= {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 13 ({{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}) |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (parallel major), former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |札所等= |本殿= |例祭= October 16 |神事= |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Shiki
Nagayoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Shikinagayoshi Shrine''' (しきながよしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Nagayoshi Nagahara|Q88286573|WD=Q88286573|1=Nagayoshi-nagahara}}, {{ill|lt=Hirano-ku|Q861256|WD=Q861256|1=Hirano-ku, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}. It was formerly a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === History === Founded in {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|WD=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 13 ({{ill|lt=794|Q30887|WD=Q30887|1=794}}). When they worshiped {{ill|lt=Lycopodium clavatum|Q466426|WD=Q466426|lt=hikagekazura|1=Lycopodium clavatum}} growing east of this shrine, {{ill|lt=Emperor Heizei|Q353558|WD=Q353558|1=Emperor Heizei}} granted the divine title Hikage Daimyōjin and the imperial poem "Kamiyama no hikage no tsura kazatefu toyo no akari no wakete kumanaki." Since then, hikagekazura has been the shrine crest. It was later designated an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki inner shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}, became a prayer shrine of {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Suzaku|Q349296|WD=Q349296|1=Emperor Go-Suzaku}} and {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Reizei|Q356060|WD=Q356060|1=Emperor Go-Reizei}}, and Amida-ji was founded as its shrine temple. In the {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|WD=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}} and {{ill|lt=Sengoku period (Japan)|Q204023|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, it was called Nagahara Ōmiya. On May 6, {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|WD=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 20 ({{ill|lt=1615|Q6784|WD=Q6784|1=1615}}), after the {{ill|lt=Battle of Dōmyōji|Q1002716|WD=Q1002716|1=Battle of Dōmyōji}}, {{ill|lt=Sanada Nobushige|Q3948081|WD=Q3948081|1=Sanada Yukimura}} stopped at this shrine, prayed for victory, and rested at its horse grounds. At that time, a banner and sword were dedicated to the shrine. In the {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|WD=Q184963|1=Edo period}}, it was mainly called Hikage Daimyōjin. It enshrined {{ill|lt=Gozu Tennō|Q11570247|WD=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}} and drew worship for reputed efficacy in rain-prayer rituals. When {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|WD=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} was carried out in the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} era, the shrine temple Amida-ji was abolished, but this shrine retained the materials without dispersal. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}} (Meiji 5), it was ranked {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The sword dedicated by Sanada Nobushige was confiscated by {{ill|lt=GHQ|Q471889|WD=Q471889|lt=GHQ|1=Supreme Commander for the Allied Powers}} after {{ill|lt=World War II|Q184425|WD=Q184425|1=Pacific War}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko|Q11620922|WD=Q11620922|lt=Nagae Sotsuhiko-no-Mikoto|1=Katsuragi Sotsuhiko}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} File:志紀長吉神社 祭神・由緒.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine deities and history === Precinct=== * Honden * Haiden * {{ill|lt=Kotohira-gū|Q94760|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} - deity: {{ill|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama|Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|WD=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Ōkami|1=Ukanomitama}} * Distant worship site for {{ill|lt=Ise Jingū|Q687168|WD=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Jinmu Emperor's Mausoleum (Unebi NE mausoleum)|UNKNOWN|lt=Mausoleum of Emperor Jinmu at Unebiyama}} * Shrine office * Mikoshi storehouse File:志紀長吉神社 琴平社.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine Kotohira Shrine File:志紀長吉神社 稲荷社.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine Inari Shrine File:志紀長吉神社 遥拝所.jpg|Shikinagayoshi Shrine distant worship site === Cultural properties === === Osaka City designated tangible cultural properties === * Silk painting of {{ill|lt=Kūkai|Q222227|WD=Q222227|lt=Kōbō Daishi|1=Kūkai}} * Silk painting Gozu Tennō mandala * 11 documents of Shikinagayoshi Shrine === Transportation === * 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Nagahara Station (Osaka)|Q151508|WD=Q151508|lt=Nagahara Station|1=Nagahara Station (Osaka)}} on {{ill|lt=Osaka Metro|Q51211653|WD=Q51211653|1=Osaka Metro (company)}} {{ill|lt=Osaka Metro Tanimachi Line|Q1142773|WD=Q1142773|lt=Tanimachi Line|1=Tanimachi Line}} === Bibliography === * Shikinagayoshi Shrine origin record === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[https://www.shikinagayoshi-jinjya.org/ Shikinagayoshi Shrine] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Shikinagayoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka]] [[Category:平野区の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hirano-ku, Osaka]] [[Category:長吉]]
